story: Eager students rushed through the doors of a brand-new school in Ithaca, New York. Cornell University opened its gates for the first time in 1868, welcoming curious minds ready to learn. On October 7, this innovative college began its journey to become one of America's most respected universities. Cornell's founders dreamed of a place where anyone could study any subject they desired.
activity_question: Where was Cornell University founded?
follow_up_question: What was special about Cornell's approach to education when it opened?

title: Daring Escape by Hot Air Balloon
story: Swoosh! A hot air balloon rose into the sky, carrying a brave passenger to safety. On October 7, 1870, Leon Gambetta made a daring escape from Paris using this unusual method. The city was under siege, but Gambetta refused to give up. He floated away to continue leading France's defense from outside the capital.
activity_question: How did Leon Gambetta escape from Paris?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Gambetta chose a balloon for his escape?

title: Seeing the Invisible: A Photographic First
story: Cameras captured a whole new world in 1931. Scientists in Rochester, New York, took the first infrared photograph on October 7. This special picture showed things our eyes can't normally see. Infrared light is invisible to humans, but this breakthrough let us 'see' heat and hidden details in nature and space.
activity_question: What type of photograph was taken for the first time?
follow_up_question: How might infrared photography be useful in studying animals at night?
